title: Trochocarpa disticha
taxonomy: Trochocarpa disticha belongs to the family Ericaceae. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to Australia. This evergreen shrub is classified as a member of the genus Trochocarpa. The species is further classified within the subfamily Epacridoideae. Trochocarpa disticha is a plant of the Southern Hemisphere, particularly found in Tasmania and Australia.
uses: Trochocarpa disticha is an Australian native plant that has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people. The plant's leaves and bark are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections.
